name: @radzor/health-check
description: Health check endpoint with dependency monitoring. Register dependencies (databases, APIs, caches), run periodic checks, and expose a unified health report. Zero dependencies.

inputs.name: intervalMs
inputs.description: Interval in ms between automatic health checks.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: Timeout in ms for each individual dependency check.

inputs.name: historySize
inputs.description: Number of historical check results to retain per dependency.

outputs.name: healthReport
outputs.description: Aggregated health status of all registered dependencies.

events.name: onHealthy
events.description: Fired when all dependencies are healthy.

events.name: onUnhealthy
events.description: Fired when one or more dependencies are unhealthy.

actions.name: check
actions.description: Run all dependency health checks and return the aggregated report.

actions.name: registerDependency

params.name: checker
params.description: Async function that returns true if the dependency is healthy.

params.name: critical
params.description: If true, a failure makes the overall status 'unhealthy'. If false, status is 'degraded'.

actions.description: Register a dependency with its health check function.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the dependency to remove.

actions.name: getHistory

params.name: name
params.description: Dependency name to get history for.

actions.description: Get the historical check results for a specific dependency.

connectsTo.description: Alert when service dependencies are unhealthy

llm.constraints: Each dependency check is wrapped in a timeout — a check that takes longer than `timeout` ms is considered failed. The checker function must return a Promise<boolean>. The auto-check interval timer is unref'd and won't keep the process alive.
